topic.what: Systematic benchmarking of 10 frontier LLMs from 7 providers on 200 NYU CTF challenges using extended D-CIPHER multi-agent framework
topic.how: Controlled factorial experiments varying environment (Ubuntu vs Kali), prompting strategy (generic vs tips vs auto-prompting), model selection, and planner/executor architecture assignments
topic.why_matters: Quantifies offensive cyber capabilities of frontier LLMs and identifies primary performance drivers to inform capability assessment and responsible use

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of CTF challenges solved by LLM agent
characteristics.context: Single-trial measurement at T=1.0; Gemini 3 Pro baseline 42.5% already exceeds prior SOTA

characteristics.what_it_measures: Solve rate improvement from Kali Linux vs Ubuntu environment
characteristics.evidence: Kali 52.0% (104/200) vs Ubuntu 42.5% (85/200) under Gemini 3 Pro + Generic prompts
characteristics.context: Strongest gains in forensics (+26.7pp) and reverse engineering (+9.8pp)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Solve rate change when auto-prompting is enabled
characteristics.evidence: 52.0% → 39.5% under Kali + Generic prompts with Gemini 3 Pro
characteristics.context: Auto-prompting generally hurts performance; consumes budget without producing actionable insight

characteristics.what_it_measures: API cost per successfully solved challenge
characteristics.evidence: Gemini 3 Flash solves 27% at $0.05/solve vs Opus 59% at $2.12/solve
characteristics.context: Gemini 3 Flash offers best cost-performance tradeoff; Claude Opus best for accuracy priority

characteristics.what_it_measures: Solve rate benefit of asymmetric vs symmetric model assignments
characteristics.evidence: Pro Planner + Flash Executor: 28.5%; Flash Planner + Pro Executor: 23.5%
characteristics.context: Same-model configurations outperform mixed-tier pairings; coordination coherence matters more than raw capability

characteristics.what_it_measures: Solve rates broken down by CTF challenge category
characteristics.value: misc easiest (varies by model), pwn hardest
characteristics.vs_baseline: Claude Opus leads in all categories vs Gemini Pro
characteristics.evidence: Claude 4.5 Opus: pwn 41.0%, web 52.6%; Gemini 3 Pro: pwn 33.3%, web 47.4%
characteristics.context: Binary exploitation (pwn) consistently hardest; miscellaneous challenges easiest

good_for: benchmark design for offensive cyber LLMs
model selection for cyber agent tasks
requires: Kali Linux environment with 100+ security tools
significant computational budget for evaluation runs

novelty: First controlled study varying environment, prompting, model, and architecture on same benchmark
Kali Linux environment with tool-discovery agents yields +9.5pp improvement
Cost-performance frontier analysis across 10 frontier models from 7 providers
Demonstration that homogeneous planner/executor configurations outperform asymmetric assignments
open_problems: Generalization across other agent frameworks beyond D-CIPHER unknown
Static CTF benchmarks susceptible to data contamination; live benchmark validation needed
Whether coherence effect generalizes across providers beyond Google models
Persistent challenges in binary exploitation and forensics categories remain unsolved
